data.title: First Bancorp declares $0.23 dividend
data.description:
• First Bancorp (FBNC) declares $0.23/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Payable Jan. 25; for shareholders of record Dec. 31; ex-div Dec. 31.
  • See FBNC Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Fed's Hammack says she'd prefer 'slightly more' restrictive policy
data.description:
Federal Reserve Bank of Cleveland President Beth Hammack said Friday that she would prefer monetary policy "slightly more" restrictive than current levels, as pressures on both sides of the central bank's dual mandate persist.

The combination of a gradually cooling labor market, with payroll growth falling and the jobless rate creeping up, and inflation staying nearly a full percentage point above the 2% target complicate the policy path, Hammack, who is not a voting member of the rate-setting Federal Open Market Committee this year, repeated in a fireside chat at an event hosted by the University of Cincinnati Real Estate Center.

"To me, right now, we have policy that is right around neutral," Hammack said. "And when we've got challenges on both sides of our mandate, being close to neutral is probably the right place to be."

She added that she would prefer a "slightly more restrictive" policy stance to continue to help ease inflation pressures. The Fed has cut rates by 75 basis points this year, with the last move in the past week marking the third straight quarter-point reduction.

This is a developing story. Check back for updates.

data.title: ASML CEO delves into Nvidia, China and more in lengthy interview
data.description:
For ASML (ASML) CEO Christophe Fouquet, who took the job in 2024, his next big test is whether he can lead a transition from what’s known as extreme ultraviolet lithography, or EUV, technology to high numerical aperture, or High NA, EUV. 

ASML will work with its customers on getting the High NA machines to the point they can operate with minimal downtime through next year. Fouquet expects high-volume manufacturing in 2027 and 2028, Bloomberg News reported, citing an interview in the company’s boardroom.

Sometime next decade, ASML will introduce an even more advanced technology called Hyper NA.

The company is tapping into two powerful industry trends. First, semiconductor demand continues to climb as chips find their way into automobiles, consumer devices, and many products outside the usual computing and smartphone markets.

Then, with the debut of ChatGPT in 2022, tech giants have accelerated efforts to build AI data centers packed with the most advanced processors (mainly GPUs) and memory chips, making ASML's (ASML) highest-end gear even more vital.

The global semiconductor market is projected to rise 22% to $772B this year and more than 25% to $975 billion next year, according to the World Semiconductor Trade Statistics organization.

Intel (INTC) and other customers are experimenting with the new machines, which would make chips even more capable of running advanced applications in AI and other fields. Fouquet says they are slowly making their way toward commercial production, the report added.

Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM) executive Kevin Zhang said in May that the foundry giant, which accounts for more than half of global semiconductor production, was still internally debating the use of ASML high numerical aperture machines for future semiconductor nodes.

“The lithography improvements that we designed the tool for have been demonstrated,” he says. “The imaging is very good, the resolution is very good. Now we're working with them to finalize the maturity of the system.”

The report further noted that ASML’s combination of unique technology, locked-in suppliers, and loyal customers has prompted some analysts to conclude that Fouquet has the most secure monopoly in the semiconductor business.

And while ASML does have rivals, they’re still far behind.

While Shanghai Micro Electronics Equipment has received Chinese government backing to help the country catch up in lithography, its machines lag ASML’s by 10 to 15 years.

“Are we going to see people trying to do lithography? Of course,” Fouquet says, as he nears the end of the interview in Veldhoven. “But that’s very difficult. And the entire ecosystem is very, very dependent. So it’s not only about lithography; it’s also how our lithography is integrated in the entire process at our customers.”

“We mostly know what to do for our customers for the next 10-to-15 years,” he says. “Better lithography means better resolution, better accuracy, and better productivity. And we're going to work basically on those three axes for many, many years.” 

Fouquet further added that while the traditional pace in the chip industry has been to double the number of transistors on a chip every two years—this is Moore’s Law—Nvidia (NVDA) wants to move even faster. “They would like the number of transistors to go up by a factor of 16 every two years,” Fouquet says. “So you're going completely off Moore's Law.”

In China, meanwhile, Fouquet has to navigate the complexities of geopolitics.

The country was the biggest market for ASML last year as Beijing pushed to build up its domestic chip industry, but the company is banned from selling all EUV machines and its most advanced DUV machines into the country.

“China will not accept to be cut off from technology,” Fouquet says. “This is just reality.”

data.title: Nasdaq 100 futures drop as Broadcom guidance puts AI trade under pressure
data.description:
Stock index futures pointed to a mixed open Friday, as AI momentum hit another speed bump.

Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) fell by -0.29% to 25,612.99 points. Dow futures (INDU) rose by +0.31% to 48,853.00 points, while S&P 500 futures (SPX) edged higher by +0.10% to 6,908.01 points.

The AI trade came under pressure after chipmaker Broadcom (AVGO) warned of margin pressures and disappointed investors with a lesser-than-expected backlog figure for AI product orders. 

"The swing in AI sentiment continued with Broadcom’s (AVGO) earnings after the US close," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said. "Following a +78% advance YTD, the chipmaker now has a larger market cap than Meta and Tesla, playing a growing role in the tech market narrative." 

"The stock initially advanced +4% after-hours after unveiling stronger-than-expected revenue guidance for the current quarter ($19.1bn vs $18.5bn est.). But it then turned sharply lower as management held off on giving an AI revenue forecast for the year," Reid added.

In the Treasury market, yields showed varied movements across different maturities. The U.S. 2-Year Treasury yield (US2Y) decreased by -0.17% to 3.55%, while the 10-Year Treasury yield (US10Y) moved in the opposite direction, rising +0.22% to 4.17%. The longer-dated 30-Year yield (US30Y) was up +0.44% to 4.82%.

Top gainers in premarket trading included lululemon athletica (LULU) +9.69%, Bristol Myers Squibb (BMY) +3.05%, and West Pharmaceutical Services (WST) +2.73%. Notable decliners were Broadcom (AVGO) -4.77%, UDR (UDR) -3.28%, and CoStar Group (CSGP) -2.82%.

data.title: Gilead and Arcus halt late-stage study for anti-TIGIT cancer drug
data.description:
Arcus Biosciences (RCUS) fell ~14% in the premarket on Friday after the company announced the discontinuation of a Phase 3 trial designed to evaluate its anti-TIGIT therapy domvanalimab in gastric and esophageal cancers as part of a collaboration with Gilead Sciences (GILD).

The STAR-221 study evaluated domvanalimab with anti-PD-1 therapy zimberelimab and chemotherapy as a first-line option versus chemotherapy plus nivolumab, an anti-PD-1 immunotherapy marketed by Bristol Myers (BMY) as Opdivo.

Citing a pre-specified interim analysis, Arcus (RCUS) said that an independent data monitoring panel determined that relative to the off-drug arm, the domvanalimab-based regimen has not improved overall survival, the primary endpoint in the 1,040-patient global trial.

The experimental therapy showed a safety profile similar to nivolumab plus chemotherapy, and there were no new safety issues, the company noted.

However, RCUS said it would discontinue its Phase 2 EDGE-Gastric studies in addition to STAR-221 due to the setback and instead focus its resources on casdatifan, another cancer drug for which multiple data readouts and study initiations are expected next year.

Arcus (RCUS) also reported roughly $1B of cash and investments, which it said would be enough to fund its operations through H2 2028.

Stocks to watch include other notable developers of anti-TIGIT therapies, such as Compugen (CGEN).

data.title: Silynxcom secures $3M order from Asian military customer
data.description:
• Silynxcom (SYNX) on Friday said it has received a purchase order valued at over $3 million from a prominent military customer in Asia.
  • The order encompasses a variety of Silynxcom's products, including its flagship tactical in-ear headset system.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Retail sales rose 4.5% in November as the holiday season starts solid - NRF
data.description:
Retail sales were up 0.1% seasonally adjusted month over month and up 4.5% unadjusted year over year in November, according to the National Retail Federation. That compared with increases of 0.6% month over month and 5.0% year over year in October.

The Retail Monitor calculation of core retail sales (excluding restaurants in addition to auto dealers and gas stations) was down a slight 0.04% month over month in November but up 4.7% year over year. Total sales were up 5.1% year over year for the first 11 months of the year, and core sales were up 5.2%.

"Retail sales showed healthy year-over-year gains in November, while month-over-month data was largely flat," stated National Retail Federation CEO Matthew Shay. "Shoppers looking for online deals may have held back a bit until Cyber Monday, which landed in December due to a late Thanksgiving, likely shifting some spending. Consumers are focusing on value and spending carefully during the holiday period, and retailers are offering products at competitive prices to fit every budget," he added.

The November results come as the National Retail Federation is forecasting that 2025 holiday sales from November 1 through December 31 will increase between 3.7% and 4.2% compared with 2024 to just over $1 trillion.

data.title: Chicago Fed's Goolsbee: Waiting for more economic data before lowering rates would have been prudent
data.description:
Chicago Federal Reserve President Austan Goolsbee said on Friday that a more prudent course would have been to wait for more data before lowering rates further.

As widely expected, the FOMC reduced its benchmark interest rate by 25 basis points on Wednesday, its third straight cut.

The decision showed an increased number of dissents, with three voting members disagreeing with the majority. 

Goolsbee had voted to lower rates at the September and October meetings, but dissented on the decision to cut the federal funds rate at the December 10 Federal Open Market Committee meeting.

"I believe we should have waited to get more data, especially about inflation, before lowering rates further," said Chicago Fed President Austan Goolsbee.

According to Goolsbee, waiting to take this matter up in 2026 meant less additional risk and the added benefit of updated economic data, which was absent lately.

He said inflation has been above their target for four and a half years, and most of the data they have show stable economic growth with a labor market only moderately cooling.

"If the labor market were deteriorating rapidly, it would be a different calculation," said Goolsbee.

"Before the data went silent, there were some concerning readings. Fortunately, we will find out important information about these risks over the next few months and, hopefully, that can provide confidence we are on a path back to 2% inflation," the Chicago Fed President said.

"I remain optimistic that interest rates can come down a significant amount over the next year. As I have reiterated for months, my unease is about too heavily front-loading rate cuts and just assuming that inflation will be transitory," added Goolsbee.

data.title: Micron in focus as Stifel ups target ahead of earnings
data.description:
• Micron (MU) was in focus on Friday as Stifel upped its price target on the memory maker ahead of its next quarterly earnings report, set for Dec. 17.
  • Shares fell fractionally in premarket trading.
  • “Memory has been on a tear,” analysts at the firm wrote in a note to clients. “Further strengthening in AI infrastructure demand has proven a tipping point, and memory prices are rallying hard into yr-end. Next week, we expect Micron to report and guide meaningful to the upside to its F1Q(Nov) results and F2Q(Feb) outlook.”
  • The analysts continued: “Buyer capitulation is a good thing. Yet, the classic cyclical concern is capacity additions/supply growth accelerating to capitalize on current conditions. As yet, we believe suppliers are instead prioritizing 1) full support of higher-value cloud memory products/customers, while also 2) re-establishing a higher, more profitable price/Gb for smartphone and client-PC products.”
  • The analysts reiterated their Buy rating on Micron and upped their price target to $300 from $195.
  • A consensus of analysts expect Micron to earn $3.90 per share on $12.82B in revenue.

data.title: GSK gets EU backing to market Arexvy RSV shot for all adults
data.description:
An expert panel of the European Medicines Agency ((EMA)) has endorsed expanding the marketing authorization for Arexvy, a vaccine developed by GSK (GSK) against respiratory syncytial virus ((RSV)) for those aged 18 years and older, the British drugmaker announced on Friday.

Issuing a positive opinion, the EMA’s Committee for Medicinal Products for Human Use ((CHMP)) has recommended the authorization, paving the way for the European Commission to take a final decision on the approval in February.

Developed by GSK using an adjuvant licensed from Agenus (AGEN), Arexvy, is currently available in the EU and the U.S. for people aged 60 and above and for adults aged 50-59 who are at high risk for RSV disease, for the prevention of lower respiratory tract disease caused by the virus.

The company said that efforts are underway to expand the vaccine’s indications in other regions, including the U.S. and Japan.
